title: Customer Data Handling Basics
description: Test your ability to recognize safe and unsafe ways to handle customer and employee information.

questions.prompt: Why is customer data handling a cybersecurity issue?

choices.text: Because only cybersecurity staff can view customer records
Because exposed, misused, or poorly stored data can be accessed by unauthorized people
Because all business files are public by default
Because customer data only matters during tax season

questions.explanation: Poor data handling can expose information to unauthorized people and create privacy, fraud, and security risks.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of sensitive data a small business should protect?

choices.text: Customer contact information
Employee payroll records
Account login credentials
All of the above

questions.explanation: Customer data, employee records, and credentials can all create serious security and privacy risks if exposed.

questions.prompt: It is safe to store business files containing customer information on a personal device if it is more convenient.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Sensitive business data should be stored only in approved systems and locations, not on personal devices unless specifically authorized.

questions.prompt: What is one reason access to sensitive data should be limited?

choices.text: It reduces unnecessary exposure and lowers risk
It makes files impossible to use
It removes the need for passwords
It means no one can do their job

questions.explanation: Limiting access helps reduce the number of people who can accidentally or intentionally expose sensitive information.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a common data handling mistake?

choices.text: Double-checking the recipient before sending a file
Using approved business storage systems
Sending sensitive information to the wrong email recipient
Reporting suspicious access quickly

questions.explanation: Sending files to the wrong recipient is a common and preventable cause of data exposure.

questions.prompt: HIPAA is an example of a regulation related to protecting what kind of information?

choices.text: Medical and health information
Public marketing materials
Shipping schedules only
Printer settings

questions.explanation: HIPAA is associated with the protection of medical and health information handled by covered healthcare entities.

questions.prompt: Scenario: You are about to email a spreadsheet with customer contact details and billing notes. What should you do first?

choices.text: Send it quickly so you do not forget
Double-check the recipient and make sure the sharing method is appropriate
Forward it to several coworkers in case someone else needs it
Move it to your personal phone before sending

questions.explanation: Before sending sensitive information, verify the recipient and use approved, appropriate sharing methods.

questions.prompt: A data exposure incident always requires a hacker to break into the network.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Many exposures happen through everyday mistakes like oversharing, wrong recipients, or lost devices.

questions.prompt: Which habit best helps protect customer and employee data?

choices.text: Reusing the same password across systems
Sharing folders with everyone for convenience
Using strong passwords and MFA where available
Storing files wherever there is extra space

questions.explanation: Strong passwords and MFA are important protections against unauthorized access to sensitive business data.

questions.prompt: What should you do if sensitive data is sent to the wrong person or accessed unexpectedly?

choices.text: Ignore it unless someone complains
Try to fix it quietly and not tell anyone
Report it promptly through your organization's process
Delete your own copy and assume the problem is solved

questions.explanation: Mistaken sharing or suspicious access should be reported quickly so the organization can respond and reduce harm.
